content:
What is a BPS state? In supersymmetric theories a BPS state saturates the bound
$$M = |Z(\gamma)|$$where $Z(\gamma)$ is the central charge evaluated on the charge vector $\gamma \in \Gamma$. BPS states are annihilated by half the supercharges:
$$\bar{Q}_{\dot\alpha}|\text{BPS}\rangle = 0$$Wall-crossing A wall of marginal stability $\mathcal{W}(\gamma_1,\gamma_2)$ is defined by
$$\operatorname{Im}\!\left(\frac{Z(\gamma_1)}{Z(\gamma_2)}\right) = 0$$Crossing it, a state with charge $\gamma = \gamma_1 + \gamma_2$ can decay. The Kontsevich–Soibelman formula tracks how BPS indices jump:
$$\prod_{\substack{\gamma\,\text{active} \\ \text{side A}}} \mathcal{K}_\gamma^{\Omega(\gamma)} = \prod_{\substack{\gamma\,\text{active} \\ \text{side B}}} \mathcal{K}_\gamma^{\Omega(\gamma)}$$Code example import numpy as np def central_charge(gamma, tau): """Compute Z(gamma) at modulus tau.""" n, m = gamma return n + m * tau
summary: Introduction to BPS states and the Kontsevich–Soibelman wall-crossing formula.
title: BPS states and wall-crossing

who is meowsicist? Physics graduate working at the intersection of theoretical physics and quantum computing. Currently pursuing research opportunities in string theory, lattice QFT, and quantum algorithms.
interests quantum computing — FPGA acceleration, VQE, QAOA, error mitigation theoretical physics — lattice QFT, gauge theories, AdS/CFT, string theory mathematics — analysis, topology, Lie groups, algebraic geometry $$|\text{meowsicist}\rangle = \alpha\,|\text{physics}\rangle + \beta\,|\text{math}\rangle + \gamma\,|\text{qc}\rangle$$
